metaTitle: Egress Window Installation in Crete, NE | Lincoln Egress
metaDescription: Egress window installation and window well service in Crete, NE. Basement bedroom code compliance for rentals near Doane. Free estimate: (509) 351-8404.
heroSub: Egress window installation and window well work for Crete homes, from pre-war houses near downtown to rental properties serving Doane University students and Saline County workers.
introHtml:
Crete sits about 25 miles southwest of Lincoln on Highway 33, and we handle egress window installation in Crete for homeowners and landlords across Saline County. A lot of Crete's housing stock predates modern building code, and basements along the streets near downtown and the Big Blue River were often finished for bedrooms without a proper emergency exit.

With Doane University drawing student renters and the local workforce keeping demand high for affordable housing, basement bedrooms are common in Crete rentals, and each one needs a code-compliant egress window to legally count as a sleeping room. We install and replace egress windows and window wells sized to Nebraska building code, with free written estimates so you know the cost before we start. Call (509) 351-8404 to schedule a look at your basement.

services.title: Egress Window Installation
services.desc: New egress openings cut into Crete basement walls for homeowners finishing a basement bedroom or landlords bringing a rental up to code before it's leased again.

services.title: Egress Window Replacement
services.desc: Swapping out undersized or non-compliant basement windows in Crete's older homes for units that meet the minimum opening size Nebraska code requires for a legal bedroom exit.

services.title: Window Well Installation
services.desc: Galvanized or composite wells set with proper drainage for Crete's clay-heavy soil, so the window works as an escape route and doesn't fill with water after a storm off the Big Blue.

services.title: Basement Window Replacement
services.desc: Standard basement window swaps for Crete homes that aren't adding a bedroom but need a tighter, better-draining window in an aging foundation opening.

sections.heading: Egress code compliance for Crete rental properties
sections.html:
Crete's rental market runs on two groups: Doane University students looking for affordable off-campus housing and workers tied to the local plants and businesses that anchor the town's economy. Both groups fill basement bedrooms, and Nebraska rental code doesn't allow a basement room to be advertised or used as a bedroom unless it has a code-sized egress window.

We see this most often in older Crete houses that were divided into rentals decades ago, long before egress requirements were enforced the way they are now. A basement window that's too small, painted shut, or blocked by an undersized well won't pass a rental inspection, and it puts tenants at real risk if there's ever a fire.

Our egress window installation process is the same whether the home is owner-occupied or a rental: we cut the opening to meet minimum width, height, and net clear opening requirements, then finish with a well sized to match. Landlords get a written estimate up front, so there's no surprise when it's time to close on financing or list a unit.

sections.heading: Working with Crete's older housing stock
sections.html:
A good number of Crete homes near downtown and along the older residential streets date back to the early and mid-1900s, built when basement windows were meant for light and ventilation, not emergency exit. Foundations from that era often have narrow window openings set high in the wall, which makes a straight window swap impossible without enlarging the opening itself.

That kind of work means cutting concrete or block, which is a bigger job than a standard basement window replacement, but it's what actually gets an old opening up to code instead of just installing a slightly bigger window that still falls short. We size every cut to the specific egress window going in, not a generic template, since older Crete foundations rarely match modern standard dimensions exactly.

If your home still has its original basement windows, we can tell you during a free estimate whether an enlargement is needed or whether a straight egress window replacement will clear code as-is.

sections.heading: Serving Crete and Saline County
sections.html:
Crete is the Saline County seat, and we work throughout town and the surrounding rural properties, not just inside city limits. That includes acreages and farmhouses outside Crete proper where basement bedrooms are just as common as they are in town, and where window well drainage matters even more given the flatter, slower-draining ground typical of the area.

We're based out of Lincoln and make the drive down Highway 33 regularly, so scheduling a Crete job doesn't mean a longer wait than a job inside Lincoln. See typical project pricing on our egress window cost page, or compare notes with our work in nearby Seward and Beatrice.

Whether it's one egress window for a single rental bedroom or a full basement finish with two or three windows, we give the same free written estimate and licensed, insured install on every Crete job.

faq.q: Do Doane University rental houses in Crete need egress windows?
faq.a: If a basement room in a Crete rental is used or advertised as a bedroom, it needs a code-compliant egress window regardless of who's renting it. This applies to houses near campus that landlords have converted for student housing just as much as long-term family rentals. We install and size the window to meet the net clear opening Nebraska code requires.

faq.q: How much does egress window installation cost in Crete?
faq.a: Pricing in Crete runs similar to our Lincoln jobs, generally in the range covered on our egress window cost page, since the biggest cost driver is whether we're cutting a brand-new opening or replacing an existing window. Older Crete foundations that need the opening enlarged for code compliance run toward the higher end. We give an exact number after seeing the basement in person, free of charge.

faq.q: Does Crete's clay soil affect window well drainage?
faq.a: Yes. Like much of southeast Nebraska, the soil around Crete drains slowly, so a window well without a proper gravel base and drainage path can fill with standing water after heavy rain. We build every well with compacted gravel below the sill and tie into existing drain tile where it's available, which matters more on flat lots near the Big Blue River.

faq.q: How far is Crete from your Lincoln office?
faq.a: Crete is about 25 miles southwest of Lincoln, roughly a 30 to 35 minute drive down Highway 33. We schedule Crete jobs on the same timeline as work inside Lincoln, so distance doesn't mean a longer wait for an estimate or install.
